paper_title: Late Pleistocene and Holocene groundwater flow history in the Baltic Artesian Basin: a synthesis of numerical models and hydrogeochemical data

methods.method_name: Groundwater Data Compilation and Database Creation
methods.description: Compilation of a comprehensive database containing isotopic, chemical, and dissolved gas composition of groundwater samples collected from private, water supply, and observation wells in Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania over several decades (1974-2017). This database serves as a foundation for studying the geochemical evolution, origin, and age of groundwater in the Baltic Artesian Basin.

steps.description: Collect groundwater samples from both private, water supply and observation wells during the period of 1974-2017.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect groundwater samples from various well types over a long period

steps.description: Analyze groundwater samples for chemical composition (major ions, minor ions, trace elements, alkalinity).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze groundwater samples for chemical parameters
outputs: Chemical composition data (major ions, minor ions, trace elements, alkalinity)

steps.description: Analyze groundwater samples for isotopic composition (stable isotope ratios of δ2H water, δ18O water, δ13C DIC, δ34S SO4; activities of 226Ra, 228Ra; 14C and 3H; stable isotope ratios of δ13C CH4, δ2H CH4, δ13C CO2).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze groundwater samples for isotopic composition
outputs: Isotopic composition data (δ2H, δ18O, δ13C, δ34S, 226Ra, 228Ra, 14C, 3H)

steps.description: Analyze groundwater samples for dissolved gas composition (noble gas concentrations of He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe; the ratios of 3He/4He, 36Ar/40Ar, 81Kr/85Kr; CO2 and CH4 concentrations).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze groundwater samples for dissolved gas composition
outputs: Dissolved gas data (noble gas concentrations, noble gas ratios, CO2, CH4 concentrations)

steps.description: Compile analyzed chemical, isotopic, and dissolved gas data into a database (now published as Vaikmäe et al. 2020).
steps.searchable_summary: Compile analyzed groundwater data into a database

methods.description: Method used in previous studies to determine groundwater ages, particularly on million-year timescales, involving the measurement of 81Kr and noble gases, and specifically determining 81Kr/Kr and 85Kr/Kr isotope ratios using the Atom Trap Trace Analysis (ATTA) instrument.

steps.description: Collect groundwater samples suitable for noble gas and krypton analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect groundwater samples for noble gas and krypton analysis
inputs: Specialized sampling equipment (e.g., for dissolved gases)

steps.description: Measure 81Kr and noble gases in the deepest aquifer system of the BAB.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure 81Kr and noble gas concentrations/ratios in groundwater samples
outputs: Noble gas concentrations (He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe)

steps.description: Determine the isotope ratios 81Kr/Kr and 85Kr/Kr using the ATTA3 instrument in the Laboratory for Radiokrypton Dating, Argonne National Laboratory.
Use measured data (81Kr, noble gases, 81Kr/Kr, 85Kr/Kr) to determine groundwater ages on the one million-year timescale.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate groundwater age from radiokrypton and noble gas data

methods.method_name: Methane Isotope Analysis for Origin Study
methods.description: Method used in previous studies to study the origin and formation pathways of methane in groundwater of glacial origin, specifically involving the stable isotope analysis of methane (δ13C CH4 and δ2H CH4).

steps.description: Collect groundwater samples containing dissolved methane from glacial origin areas (e.g., CVAS).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect groundwater samples containing methane from specific sources
inputs: Specialized sampling equipment (e.g., for dissolved gases)

steps.description: Perform isotope analysis of methane for δ13C CH4.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure δ13C isotope ratio of methane

steps.description: Perform isotope analysis of methane for δ2H CH4.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure δ2H isotope ratio of methane

steps.description: Use δ13C CH4 and δ2H CH4 values to study the origin and formation of methane in groundwater.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret methane isotope data to determine origin and formation processes
outputs: Interpretation of methane origin (e.g., microbial, thermogenic) and formation pathways

methods.method_name: Numerical Modeling of Palaeorecharge and Groundwater Flow
methods.description: Development and application of numerical models used in previous studies to simulate groundwater flow systems, specifically focusing on assessing palaeorecharge conditions under past ice sheets (e.g., Fennoscandian ice sheet) and their impact on regional groundwater flow dynamics.

steps.description: Develop a conceptual model of the groundwater flow system, including geological structure, hydrogeological properties, and boundary conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Develop conceptual hydrogeological model for numerical simulation

steps.description: Construct a numerical model based on the conceptual model, selecting appropriate software and discretization.
Incorporate parameters representing palaeorecharge conditions under the Fennoscandian ice sheet.
steps.searchable_summary: Define palaeorecharge boundary conditions in numerical model
outputs: Numerical model with palaeorecharge boundary conditions

steps.description: Run numerical simulations to assess palaeorecharge under the Fennoscandian ice sheet and its impact on regional groundwater flow.
steps.searchable_summary: Run groundwater flow simulations under palaeorecharge conditions
inputs: Numerical model setup with boundary conditions
outputs: Simulation results (e.g., head distribution, flow paths, fluxes)

steps.description: Analyze simulation results to understand groundwater flow history and the impact of palaeorecharge.
